title: Matrix (single-choice grid)
description: matrix — a grid where each row is a single-choice question over a shared set of columns. The answer is an object keyed by row value, e.g. {"price": "agree", "quality": "neutral"}. The warehouse decomposes a matrix into one sub-question per row (stable_name "satisfaction.price", "satisfaction.quality", …); the chosen column resolves to that sub-question's option_key, so a matrix lands in the star schema exactly like a set of single-selects. Rows and columns must carry unique identifiers — the publish gate rejects a missing or duplicate row id (a row id becomes part of a sub-question's identity).

elements.name: satisfaction
elements.title: How much do you agree?
elements.description: Rows are the sub-questions; the shared columns are the options each row chooses from. A scalar column ("agree") and an object column ({"value": "na", "text": "N/A"}) are both fine — same normalization as single-select choices.
